entity.summary: Centralized cryptocurrency exchange known for trans-fee mining. It paused trading and withdrawals in February 2020 after disclosing a large asset-liability shortfall.
entity.notes: Death date follows the public insolvency disclosure and paused trading/withdrawal services. CoinMarketCap also flags impeded withdrawals due to insolvency.

events.title: FCoin launched
events.description: FCoin launched in 2018 and became known for a trans-fee mining model that generated very high reported trading volumes.
events.notes: Month-level launch date from public reporting.

events.title: Trading and withdrawals paused after insolvency disclosure
events.description: FCoin paused trading and withdrawal services after disclosing that its asset reserve was short of liabilities by an estimated 7,000 to 13,000 BTC.
events.notes: Used as death_date because the platform paused trading and withdrawals and later remained inactive.

evidence.title: Crypto Exchange FCoin Insolvent After Revealing Up to $130M Bitcoin Shortfall
evidence.notes: Used for launch context, trans-fee mining context, and former Huobi CTO founder context.

evidence.title: Crypto Exchange FCoin Insolvent After Revealing Up to $130M Bitcoin Shortfall
evidence.notes: Primary report for paused trading and withdrawals, asset-liability shortfall, and 7,000 to 13,000 BTC estimate.

evidence.title: FCoin trade volume and market listings
evidence.notes: Database reference for untracked status and reported impeded withdrawals due to insolvency.

evidence.title: FCoin official site archive
evidence.notes: Used for historical domain preservation because the original exchange site is no longer operating normally.
